tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` **Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57069) by @live1206 ## What Changed The package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec, upgrading the API version to 2026-03-01. This added several new custom resource types and supporting models: - `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` — manage AI Foundry Projects under a Cognitive Services account - `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` / `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` — manage capability hosts scoped to a project - `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` — data model shared by account- and project-scoped capability hosts - `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` / `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` — new model types - `CognitiveServicesExtensions` — new extension methods on `ArmClient` and `SubscriptionResource` ## Gaps Found - **README "Key concepts" section is empty of service content** — it links to the generic Azure SDK docs page but does not describe any of the resource types in this package (accounts, projects, capability hosts, connections, deployments, etc.) - **README "Examples" section has no inline code snippets** — it only links to `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`. The `tests/Samples/` folder contains 35+ generated sample files (including new ones for `CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, `CognitiveServicesConnection`) but none are wired into the README via `Snippet:` placeholders - **CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is incomplete** — only records "Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01" without listing the new resource types (`CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, capability host data models, new extension methods) added by the migration
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Update `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to enumerate new resource types: ```markdown ## 1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01. - Added `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` to manage AI Foundry Projects nested under a Cognitive Services account. - Added `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` and `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` to manage capability hosts scoped to a project. - Added `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` as the shared data model for both account-level and project-level capability hosts. - Added `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` and `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` model types. - Added `GetCognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` extension method on `ArmClient`. ### Breaking Changes ### Bugs Fixed ### Other Changes ``` #### 1b. Edit `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` Replace the generic **Key concepts** section with service-specific content, and add `Snippet:` placeholders to the **Examples** section. See Step 2 below for the snippet details. Replace the "Key concepts" section body: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### CognitiveServicesAccountResource Represents a top-level Azure Cognitive Services account. Accounts hold AI models, deployments, connections, projects, and capability hosts. ### CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource Represents a model deployment within an account. Use this to create, update, or delete deployments of AI models. ### CognitiveServicesProjectResource Represents an AI Foundry Project nested within a Cognitive Services account. Projects can have their own connections and capability hosts. ### CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource / CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource Represents a capability host attached either to an account or to a project. Capability hosts define the AI capabilities (e.g., storage, vector search) available to the parent resource. ### Thread safety We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads. ### Additional concepts [Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) | [Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) | [Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) | [Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) | [Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) | [Mocking](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/unit-testing-mocking) | [Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/) ``` Replace the "Examples" section body: ```markdown ## Examples The following examples show common operations using the management library. ### List all Cognitive Services accounts in a subscription ```C# Snippet:CognitiveServices_GetAccounts ``` ### Create or update an account ```C# Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount ``` ### Create a project within an account ```C# Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateProject ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The repository uses a snippet-generation tool: write sample code in a test file with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`, add a placeholder in the README, then run `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject the code. #### Snippet 1 — List accounts: `CognitiveServices_GetAccounts` **Test file to create:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample_ReadmeSnippets.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Samples { public partial class Sample_ReadmeSnippets { [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task GetAccountsAsync() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_GetAccounts TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (CognitiveServicesAccountResource account in subscription.GetCognitiveServicesAccountsAsync()) { Console.WriteLine(account.Data.Name); } #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task CreateAccountAsync() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); CognitiveServicesAccountCollection accounts = resourceGroup.GetCognitiveServicesAccounts(); CognitiveServicesAccountData accountData = new CognitiveServicesAccountData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")) { Kind = "OpenAI", Sku = new Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models.CognitiveServicesSku("S0"), }; ArmOperation operation = await accounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync( Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, "myAccount", accountData); CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created account: {account.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task CreateProjectAsync() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateProject TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = await resourceGroup .GetCognitiveServicesAccountAsync("myAccount"); CognitiveServicesProjectCollection projects = account.GetCognitiveServicesProjects(); CognitiveServicesProjectData projectData = new CognitiveServicesProjectData( new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")); ArmOperation operation = await projects.CreateOrUpdateAsync( Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, "myProject", projectData); CognitiveServicesProjectResource project = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created project: {project.Data.Name}"); #endregion } } } ``` **README placeholders** — the three `Snippet:` fences added in Step 1b will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after the test file is committed. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` is missing or incomplete in these sections: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | None | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific content (see Step 1b) | | Examples | ⚠️ No inline snippets | Add `Snippet:` placeholders (see Step 1b and Step 2) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | None | | Next steps | ✅ Present | None | | Contributing | ✅ Present | None | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` — injects snippet code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitiveservices` — regenerates public API listing files (no new public types are being added by the docs changes, so this is a no-op validation) 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` — ensures consistent code formatting in the new sample file
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [CognitiveServices] Docs: Document new resource types added in TypeSpec migration (v1.6.0-beta.1)
